name: Cheese and Chile Squares
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces Parmesan cheese, cut into several pieces
2 cans (4-ounce) mild or hot green chiles
recipeInstructions:
1. Using the metal blade, drop Parmesan cheese pieces through the feed tube with the motor running and process until finely grated. Remove and set aside.
2. Chop chiles using the metal blade without washing the work bowl. Remove and set aside.
3. Use the shredding disc to process Cheddar and Monterey Jack cheeses separately. Remove and set aside.
4. Process salt, Tabasco sauce, 2 eggs, flour, baking powder, and grated Parmesan cheese with the metal blade. Add melted butter through the feed tube while running the machine. Remove the cover and add cottage cheese, then process until smooth.
5. Add 3 eggs through the feed tube, remove 2/3 of the mixture for Cheddar and chiles, and combine. Add remaining eggs and Monterey Jack cheese to the work bowl, processing 20-30 seconds.
6. Combine both mixtures and spread in a buttered 17x10 inch jelly roll pan.
7. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes, then reduce temperature to 350°F (175°C) and bake an additional 34-40 minutes until the cheese is set and firm. Cut into squares and serve warm with your favorite salsa.
Note: If using a large capacity machine, add all eggs at once and both cheeses in the processor bowl before processing.
